item.title: a work in progress
item.description:
Starting somewhere is better than nowhere.

The urge to push out a fully polished site definitely exists. It often feels as though society requires us to present a realized and finished state of ourselves in every aspect of life. Here that is not the case.

If you’re looking at this site shortly after March 10th, 2026, you’ll see this site in true infancy. I’m choosing to publish things as I go rather than feeling pressured to churn out a bunch of half baked site-filler for the sake of making it feel ‘complete’. Aside from ‘complete’ not even being an idea I ascribe to, I’m confident this place will become what it needs in it’s own time.

item.title: Pasaquan
item.description:
Pasaquan is an art site in Buena Vista, GA (pronounced byoona vih-stah). The site was created by an unusual man by the name of Eddie Owens Martin who later became known as St. EOM (pronounced saint oam). The site has undergone many restoration projects over the years lead by the Kohler Foundation, Columbus State University, and the Pasaquan Preservation Society.

In 2024 Joshua-Michéle Ross brought his Séance to Summon A Garden to Pasaquan. He worked with locals to perform an orchestral séance built with audio recordings from within the Chattahoochee Valley. You can listen to the performance at Pasaquan in Dalton’s journal article the chattahoochee beats like a heart.

item.title: Dalton Newbend
item.description:
Dalton Newbend is an artist from Dexter, GA. They received their BFA in Studio Art and minor in Art History from Columbus State University in 2020. They were also a 2019 fellow at Mildred’s Lane.
